name: Fort Billings
description_short: Fort Billings Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative established in 1936, serving over 3,600 members from Gibbstown, New Jersey with $64.8M in assets.
description_long: Fort Billings Federal Credit Union was established in 1936 and has operated as a member-owned cooperative financial institution for nearly 90 years. Headquartered in Gibbstown, New Jersey, the credit union has built its membership primarily around petroleum refining industry workers and related sectors, though membership has expanded beyond its original focus. The institution stewards over $64.8 million in assets and maintains a lean operational footprint with 1 branch location, 2 ATMs, and over 20 dedicated employees as of 2026. Fort Billings offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products and services typical of a traditional credit union. Members can access checking accounts (Share Draft with 2,100+ accounts), savings accounts (Regular Savings with 4,000+ accounts), money market accounts (100+ members), and certificates of deposit (200+ members). The credit union also provides credit cards, auto loans (new and used), personal loans, and mortgage options (both fixed-rate 15-year and longer-term options). Digital banking services include online banking, mobile app access for iOS and Android, bill pay, e-statements, and mobile payments. Additional services encompass wire transfers, financial calculators, and financial counseling. What distinguishes Fort Billings is its deep industry specialization in petroleum refining sectors and its 90-year history serving a defined membership base rather than pursuing mass-market growth. The credit union maintains member loyalty through personalized service and community connection, evidenced by stable membership of 3,600 and consistent product uptake across account types. Their mobile and online banking infrastructure appears current, with dedicated apps available on both major platforms and outage reporting capabilities for service monitoring. Honest assessment: Fort Billings is a well-established but geographically limited institution suited primarily to existing members in the petroleum refining industry and Gibbstown area. With only one physical branch and 2 ATMs, access is severely constrained for non-local members. The credit union does not appear to actively recruit new members from outside its traditional industry base, limiting relevance for general consumers seeking a new banking relationship. Mortgage availability is confirmed but detailed product information and current rates are not publicly detailed on the provided website content.
services: Mortgage loans (15-year and longer-term fixed rate)
Bill pay and online loan payments
pros: Established in 1936 with 90-year track record of financial stability and member service
Mobile app available for both iOS and Android with full banking functionality
Comprehensive loan products including auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages
Low employee-to-member ratio (20+ employees for 3,600 members) suggests personalized service
Member-owned cooperative structure with no outside shareholders
Online bill pay, e-statements, and financial counseling services included
Digital outage reporting and service status monitoring available to members
cons: Severely limited physical presence with only 1 branch and 2 ATMs in Gibbstown, NJ
Currently closed lobby hours with no posted reopening timeline visible on website
Industry-focused membership model (petroleum refining) restricts eligibility for general consumers
No reviews available on CreditDoc, making it difficult to assess member satisfaction or service quality
Limited public information on current interest rates, loan terms, and product pricing
best_for: Petroleum refining industry employees seeking banking services tailored to their sector
Gibbstown, New Jersey residents with established membership or family connections
Members who value traditional credit union cooperative principles and personal relationships
Consumers seeking mortgages and auto loans from a stable, locally-rooted institution
diagnosis: Fort Billings is best for existing members in the petroleum refining industry or those with local ties to Gibbstown, New Jersey seeking stable, relationship-based banking services. The primary caveat is that the institution does not appear to actively recruit outside its traditional membership base, and its single branch location makes it impractical for consumers outside the immediate geographic area or industry focus.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
